content:
# Paleoecology

**Summary**: The study of the interactions between organisms and their environment over geological time.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2017-amao|Amao, 2017]] used foraminifera as indicators for paleoecological reconstruction.

- [[2022-khamsin|Khamsin, 2022]] used paleoecological analysis to interpret the Dhruma formation's environment.

- [[2024-hikmahtiar|Hikmahtiar, 2024]] Referenced in this thesis.

- [[2024-alhilali|Alhilali, 2024]] used foraminifera to reconstruct paleoecological conditions in the Turoninan-Santonian.

## Key Subtopics

- [[2017-amao|Amao, 2017]] ['environmental monitoring', 'sediment-age determination']

- [[2022-khamsin|Khamsin, 2022]] used paleoecological analysis to interpret the Dhruma formation's environment.

- [[2024-hikmahtiar|Hikmahtiar, 2024]] Referenced in this thesis.

- [[2024-alhilali|Alhilali, 2024]] used foraminifera to reconstruct paleoecological conditions in the Turoninan-Santonian.

## Methods and Approaches Used

- [[2017-amao|Amao, 2017]] ['morphological analysis', 'distribution mapping']

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Foraminifera are excellent tools for interpreting ancient environments and paleoclimate reconstruction. ([[2017-amao|Amao, 2017, p. 25]])

- Lower Dhruma is interpreted as a marine environment with normal conditions. ([[2022-khamsin|Khamsin, 2022, p. 10]])

- Referenced in this thesis. ([[2024-hikmahtiar|Hikmahtiar, 2024]])

- Agglutinated benthic foraminifera were specifically targeted as a means of reconstructing paleoecological conditions. ([[2024-alhilali|Alhilali, 2024, p. 29]])

## Competing Approaches and Debates

- [[2017-amao|Amao, 2017]] understanding the impact of extreme environmental conditions on diversity
